opening_template: Start with public humiliation, then let the heroine reverse the social temperature with identity clues she does not fully explain.
season_hook_map: 20%-50%: why is Marcus Reed financing her war
50%-80%: who helped erase Elena's legal identity
80%-100%: who can survive the truth when it goes public

episode_hook_map.hook_out: A camera catches Elena's scar, the mark only the real heiress had.
A donor asks why Elena carries the West family seal.

paywall_map.beat: A witness confirms Elena knew a family protocol no outsider could know.
Marcus signs the alliance deal, then learns Elena hid part of the evidence chain.

rhythm_curve.launch_phase: false-name re-entry, first humiliation reversal, male lead enters the war
rhythm_curve.rise_phase: alliance deepens, public wins stack, evidence becomes addictive
rhythm_curve.storm_phase: betrayal, false defeat, villain pressure closes in
rhythm_curve.payoff_phase: public truth, board collapse, power without surrender

satisfaction_mix.status_reversal: Every major public event ends with Elena taking status away from Serena's camp.
satisfaction_mix.romantic_tension: Elena and Marcus weaponize closeness before they earn trust.
satisfaction_mix.truth_drop: Evidence arrives in fragments that force each episode to end on forward momentum.

villain_ladder.face: party insiders and loyal family staff

cultural_swap_rules.reason: Better fits NA-English elite public revenge taste
Makes the conflict public, modern, and legible overseas

adaptation_summary: Rebuild the source into a public-war revenge romance centered on legitimacy, spectacle, and a fast trust-threat romance axis.
adapted_plot_direction: Keep the commercial engine (hidden heir, public legitimacy war) but re-sequence reveals: open on auction-floor proof rather than charity gala beat-for-beat; move first major identity clue to EP02 donor confrontation; replace staged-accident backstory with board-sanctioned disappearance to lower chain-similarity vs source.
adapted_structure: Four-phase rhythm unchanged at macro level but information reveal order shifts: financial trail leads social wins; midpoint betrayal tied to sealed ledger not missing will trope; finale is regulatory filing + livestream not family dinner showdown.
adapted_background: Old-money East Coast philanthropy and auction houses as primary public arenas; legal identity erasure via corporate records not village gossip; board governance and donor class as social weapon.
adapted_character_system: Protagonist: Elena West (alias contract strategist) — same function as source heroine, new name and profession skin. Male lead: Marcus Reed — investor-truth seeker, colder public voice. Antagonist: Serena Cole — performative heiress, media-native villainy. All use adapted_display_name in downstream docs; stable CHAR-xx IDs unchanged.
adapted_relationships_and_turns: Elena vs Serena remains public legitimacy rivalry but first clash is auction bid war not dress insult; Elena–Marcus alliance starts as hostile term sheet then intimacy-as-leverage; storm beat is partial evidence withhold on Elena side to mirror trust fracture without copying source dialogue situations.

character_rename_map.notes: Alias public-facing name for early eps; legal reclamation name can surface later per outline
Visible usurper counterpart to Celia Hale

season_arc.goal: Establish Nora's return and first public reversals
Scale alliance pressure and evidence addiction

scene_language_rules: Keep dialogue short, sharp, and status-aware.
Every scene must change leverage, information, or public perception.
Avoid explaining the plot when the power shift can be seen.
similarity_avoidance: Shift key confrontations into public elite venues
Advance the alliance earlier than in the source
Replace household melodrama with board legitimacy warfare

similarity_score.assessment: Commercial engine retained, but conflict spaces, reveal order, and public scene design are substantially rebuilt.
